blocks.text: A2UI represents a UI tree as a **flat list**, not nested JSON. Each component has an `id` and references its children by ID. The client stores every component in a map and reconstructs the tree at render time — the spec calls this **the adjacency list model**.

blocks.title: Why flat, not nested?
blocks.text: Order stops mattering. The server can send component definitions in any sequence, and can reference a child ID that doesn't exist yet — the client buffers and fills gaps as they arrive. Exactly one component must have id "root"; until it exists, nothing renders.
A2UI ships one baseline component set, the **Basic Catalog**. Any A2UI-conformant client understands these 18 without needing anything else declared.

blocks.caption: The real Basic Catalog (catalogs/basic/catalog.json)
rows: Displays an image from a URL
A system-provided icon from a predefined list
Plays a video from a URL
A set of tabs, each with a title and child
A horizontal or vertical dividing line
A dialog over the main content
A checkbox with a label and boolean value
A field for user text input
An input for date and/or time
A numeric value within a range

blocks.text: A **catalog** is a JSON Schema file declaring exactly which components, functions, and surface properties an agent may reference. The Basic Catalog is one such file; most production systems define their own **Custom Catalog** — a design system the agent is constrained to, nothing more, nothing less.

One precise, easy-to-miss detail: `catalogId` is **a string identifier, conventionally URI-shaped, but not required to be a resolvable URI**. Client and server just need to agree out-of-band on what a given catalogId means. a2uicatalog makes its own choice here — it uses a genuinely resolvable URL (`a2uicatalog.ai/catalogue/a2ui-atoms-v1.json`) as its catalogId, so a fetching client actually *can* dereference it — a stricter, more convenient convention than the spec requires, not a spec requirement itself.

blocks.question: Per the real spec, does catalogId have to point to a real, downloadable file?
options: Yes — the spec mandates it always be dereferenceable
No — it just needs to be a string both sides agree on; URI-shaped by convention only
Only for extension components, not the Basic Catalog
Only when using the MCP transport binding
blocks.explanation: The spec is explicit: catalogId is conventionally URI-formatted to avoid naming collisions, but is not required to be resolvable. a2uicatalog's choice to make its own catalogId a real fetchable URL is a stricter convention it adopted, not a spec mandate.

points: Components are a flat list with ID references — the adjacency list model — not nested JSON
Exactly one component must carry id: "root"
The Basic Catalog is 18 named components; anything outside it needs a Custom Catalog
catalogId is a well-known string agreed out-of-band — URI-shaped by convention, not required to resolve
